No satellite lesions or similar cortical abnormalities are visible elsewhere in the imaged limb or skeleton.

Endosteal scalloping causes marked cortical thinning over the lesion while preserving overall cortical continuity.
Prominent reactive cortical thickening surrounds the lucent defect, creating a dense sclerotic rim.
Radiographs show smooth endosteal scalloping and cortical thinning without evidence of frank cortical disruption.
The anterior cortical bone appears symmetrically expanded and thinned but remains entirely intact without pathologic fracture lines.
Distinct cortical remodeling combines inner endosteal thinning with peripheral reactive sclerosis along the lesion matrix.
